spec_match.rationale: Chromium-catches-up feature — the spec already documents the behaviour, so the value of the demo is the interop story. The concept exercises one corner but the before/after comparison isn't always crisp.

interactive.rationale: Has 1 interactive control(s) wired to JS but the surface is thin — a single button or readout is barely past the static-card threshold.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Either the feature-index blurb or the concept lede is missing — can't verify alignment.

edge_cases.rationale: Some fallback / error handling is present but corner cases like removed-element behaviour, denied permission flow, or no-support browsers aren't all covered.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5073321259565056 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: right/bottom side keywords (the main new syntax)
openQuestions.detail: The side-relative-syntax-for-background-position-x-y-longhands feature's headline framing — background-position-x: right 20%; — side keyword + offset in the X/Y longhands (matching the shorthand) — calls out "right/bottom side keywords (the main new syntax)". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: animation between two side-relative values
openQuestions.detail: The side-relative-syntax-for-background-position-x-y-longhands feature's headline framing — background-position-x: right 20%; — side keyword + offset in the X/Y longhands (matching the shorthand) — calls out "animation between two side-relative values". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Add real controls so the demo isn't a styled code card
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages are supposed to be hands-on. With only 1 button(s), 0 input(s), 0 select(s), 0 range slider(s), the surface is thin. Add at least one slider, picker, or interactive readout so visitors can actually probe the API from the page.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

openQuestions.title: Align the concept lede with the feature-index blurb
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sells one promise; the concept lede frames a different angle. Either reword the lede to echo the blurb's keywords (so visitors recognise they're in the right place) or update the parent blurb.

summary: parallax-recipe under side-relative-syntax-for-background-position-x-y-longhands: 2 pass / 4 partial / 0 fail. Interactive surface is thin, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
